_doc: Allowlist for yarn audit high/critical advisories that cannot be fixed in the current branch. Every entry needs a reason and a review date. See SUPPLY-CHAIN-SECURITY.md §6.

_fields.id: npm advisory numeric ID (from `yarn audit --json` advisory.id)
_fields.ghsa: GitHub Security Advisory ID, for humans — not used for matching
_fields.reason: why the advisory is allowlisted — upstream has no fix, major version bump deferred, not on a reachable code path, etc.
_fields.added: YYYY-MM-DD when the entry was added
_fields.review: YYYY-MM-DD by which the entry must be re-evaluated. An expired entry prints a CI warning but does not fail the job.

entries.reason: No upstream patch available (npm advisory reports patched_versions: <0.0.0; the package is effectively abandoned). Reaches the tree via @solana/wallet-adapter-wallets > @solana/wallet-adapter-torus > crypto-browserify and the @solana/web3.js chain. CVE-2025-3194 is a buffer overflow in toBigIntLE(). The only way out is removing Solana support or waiting for an upstream fork. Track upstream: https://github.com/advisories/GHSA-3gc7-fjrx-p6mg.

Requires Next.js 14.2.35 → 15.x major migration (React 18 → 19, RSC API changes). Scoped to a separate PR — a Next 15 migration is not a supply-chain hardening change. Advisory is a DoS when using insecure React Server Components; apps in this monorepo use classic Next page-based routing without RSC on the hot path.
Same root cause as advisory 1112653 — Next 14 → 15 migration required. Also a RSC DoS, not RCE/credential-leak. Fixed alongside advisory 1112653 when the Next 15 migration PR lands.
